stats_fallback.christened: 2009-07-12 (Barcelona, godmother Sophia Loren, tenor José Carreras performing)
stats_fallback.cabins_note: 1,637 total including 43 accessible cabins. Original Fantasia-class layout (not the 'modified' 1,751-cabin plan used on Divina and Preziosa).
stats_fallback.builder: STX France (now Chantiers de l'Atlantique), Saint-Nazaire
stats_fallback.builder_note: STX France is period-accurate for her 4 July 2009 delivery. STX Europe had acquired Aker Yards in October 2008 — nine months before Splendida's delivery — so the yard was called STX France during her final fit-out. The yard later reverted to Chantiers de l'Atlantique in 2018.
stats_fallback.yacht_club_status: Installed from 2009 delivery. 71 suites (cabins 15001-15035 and 16001-16035 per the current deck plan). Forward enclave on Decks 14/15/18 with Concierge Area, Library, Top Sail Lounge (Deck 15), L'Olivo Restaurant (Deck 15 — shared name with sister MSC Fantasia; refreshed with a modern redesign in January-February 2026 Palumbo Malta drydock, name retained), private sundeck (Deck 18) with The One Pool, The One Bar, and the Top 18 Exclusive Solarium. Private access to a section of the MSC Aurea Spa.

related.class_notes: Fantasia Class. MSC Splendida is the second ship of the original Fantasia pair (direct sister to MSC Fantasia, both at 137,936 GT with 1,637 cabins). The later MSC Divina (2012) and MSC Preziosa (2013) form a separate 'modified Fantasia' sub-group at 139,072 GT with 1,751 cabins and a different venue programming. Each Fantasia-class ship uses unique MDR, theatre, and Yacht Club restaurant names: Splendida's are La Reggia + Villa Verde MDRs, The Strand Theatre, and the L'Olivo Yacht Club Restaurant (the L'Olivo name is shared only with sister MSC Fantasia — the modified-Fantasia ships use different Yacht Club restaurant names; Preziosa's is La Palmeraie).

deployment.spring_summer_2026: Western Mediterranean 7-night round-trips from Barcelona (Marseille, Genoa, Naples, Palermo, Tunis).
deployment.current_ais_2026_04_11: West Mediterranean approaching Barcelona for April 11, 2026 arrival (ETA 05:00 UTC per VesselFinder). Last port Tunis, Tunisia (departed April 9, 16:25 UTC).
deployment.late_summer_2026: Shifts to island-focused summer schedule adding Malta, Sicily, and Sardinia to the Barcelona/Marseille rotation.
deployment.winter_2026_27: Repositioning to South America for winter 2026-27. Homeports Rio de Janeiro and Buenos Aires for 8- and 9-night itineraries to Brazil, Argentina, and Uruguay (announced February 2026).

history.christened: 12 July 2009, Barcelona (Sophia Loren; tenor José Carreras performing)
history.class_position: Second ship of the Fantasia Class (direct sister of MSC Fantasia)
history.2023_12_south_africa_debut: December 2023 — debuted in South Africa for seasonal deployment.
history.2024_05_drydock: 10-29 May 2024 — drydock at Palumbo Shipyard, Valletta, Malta. Routine maintenance, class inspections, hull repainting. Part of MSC's six-ship 2024 drydock plan.
history.2024_25_china_to_brazil: Swapped China deployment for Brazil season.
history.2026_01_10_to_02_07_drydock: 10 January - 7 February 2026 — second Malta drydock at Palumbo Malta Shipyard, Valletta (27 days). Scope: routine maintenance, class surveys, replaced carpets and upholstery in public areas and staterooms, redesigned MSC Yacht Club Restaurant (modern layout, darker colours, soft textures, updated lighting — L'Olivo name retained), redesigned Future Cruise offices (rolled out across the Fantasia class fleet-wide). Ship resumed service 7 February 2026.

discrepancies_flagged.hallucinations_removed_from_venues_v2: Removed sea-pavilion hallucination (not on the current Splendida PDF — specialty restaurants are Butcher's Cut, Santa Fe Tex Mex, Dim Sum Bar & Tea House). Removed villa-verde-buffet hallucination (Villa Verde is a MAIN DINING ROOM on Splendida, not a buffet — and was incorrectly marked as shared with Orchestra). Removed kaito-sushi-bar from Splendida's venue list (no Kaito on the current Splendida PDF; Asian dining is at Dim Sum Bar & Tea House).
discrepancies_flagged.review_jsonld_hallucination_removed: Previous Review JSON-LD mentioned 'L'Etoile restaurant' — L'Etoile is not on MSC Splendida. Removed.
discrepancies_flagged.crew_count: Older references show 1,313 on the specs card; verified sources (MSC official, CruiseMapper) say ~1,370. Use 1,370.
discrepancies_flagged.yacht_club_suite_count: MSC primary sources and the ship's own deck plan (cabins 15001-15035 and 16001-16035) show 71 Yacht Club suites. Some older marketing pages (iCruise, RegentCruises, MSC Cruises Blog) say 99. Authoritative count is 71.
discrepancies_flagged.sisters: Splendida's direct sister is MSC Fantasia only. MSC Divina and MSC Preziosa are the 'modified Fantasia' sub-group with different GT (139,072 vs 137,936), different cabin count (1,751 vs 1,637), and different Yacht Club restaurant naming.
discrepancies_flagged.mini_golf: Not listed on Splendida's current ship map PDF. May have been removed in a previous refit.
discrepancies_flagged.eataly: Not on Splendida's current ship map PDF. The MSC-Eataly 'Ristorante Italia' partnership is not present on Splendida.
